summary:
The treatment option prioritized should address both the inability to repair the torn rotator cuff naturally and ensure full recovery of shoulder functions; total shoulder arthroplasty stands out given its capability to restore joint mechanics comprehensively post-rotator cuff failure without relying solely on tendon healing mechanisms inherent in conventional surgeries.
The optimal intervention involves opting for total shoulder replacement over other options since it restores overall shoulder function more reliably than alternatives when dealing with irreversible damage from large tears where natural tissue regeneration isn't feasible. This approach ensures better long-term outcomes regarding movement range and reduced chronic discomfort associated with significant rotator cuff pathology. To summarize concisely while adhering strictly within two-sentence limits provided,
Total shoulder replacement addresses critical needs arising from unrepaired major rotator cuff defects ensuring substantial improvement in motion & reducing persistent pain compared to alternative methods lacking similar restorative capacity under these specific conditions. Thus, this procedure emerges as superior based primarily upon achieving necessary functional gains despite permanent structural changes necessitated by advanced injury states unresponsive to conservative management strategies alone.
